summary: A two-story rectangular simple house with a stepped gable roof. The building sits centered on a shallow raised white border/pad. Exterior walls use a three-layer horizontal banding: lower and middle band of stonebrick (and cobblestone on corners), a white smooth strip between floors, and oak planks as upper band and roof. Rows of evenly spaced glass windows run around both floors.

shape.overall_form: boxy rectangular two-level structure with a symmetrical stepped gable on the short sides
shape.symmetry: mirror-symmetric along the central longitudinal axis (front/back identical), symmetrical left-right on photographed faces

materials.name: stonebrick
materials.role: wall band and window surrounds (stone brick)

materials.name: slab_stone
materials.role: white smooth horizontal band / foundation border (possible quartz or smooth stone slabs)

materials.name: slab_wood
materials.role: roof step detail (possible wood slab accents)

elements: rows of horizontal windows on both floors (evenly spaced, 1-block tall glass panes)
stepped gable roof made from oak planks with slab/step edges
white slab/foundation border around base forming a shallow platform
stonebrick bands between and below windows
rebuild_hints: Build a rectangular two-floor box ~11x9 blocks footprint. Leave a 1-block tall white slab border platform around it.

Walls: from base up: 1 row stonebrick (with cobblestone at vertical corners), 1 row windows (glass panes), 1 white smooth slab row between floors, repeat stonebrick + windows band, then an oak plank band under roof.
Roof: centered gable along the short axis using oak planks stepped up to a 3-4 block ridge with stone slab or white slab trim at the eaves and ridge.
Place window openings evenly spaced (about 6 windows across the longer face) with stonebrick full blocks beneath each window and a continuous glass strip.
Corner columns use cobblestone full blocks vertically spanning both floor bands.
uncertainties: Exact footprint dimensions: estimated from pixel views; could be off by a few blocks in width/depth.
White horizontal band and base border may be quartz blocks or smooth stone slabs rather than slab_stone; identification is uncertain.
Some small trim details (exact slab orientation, use of stairs vs slabs for roof edges) are uncertain from images.
Interior layout and presence/location of entrances are not visible in provided views.
